create an attractive portfolio, using the information from that resume.

Handling
Considering
Designing your portfolio site

Here's your portfolio:

portfolio
Artifact



I went with a warm terracotta palette (avoids the generic "tech = blue" look) and kept it clean with scroll-reveal animations, a soft organic hero shape, and a timeline-style education section. The skills display as hoverable tiles rather than boring bullets, and your interests sit as pill tags.


What's working? What isn't? I can tweak colors, add a projects section placeholder for when you build things, or change the vibe entirely.

i want some dark color pallete.

Cookin'
Considering
Rebuilding your portfolio in dark mode

Here you go:

portfolio
Artifact



Switched to a deep dark base with a teal/mint accent that glows subtly. The surfaces use a 3-tier elevation system (lighter = higher) instead of shadows, and the hero circle has a rotating conic gradient border for that extra edge. Feels moody and polished without going full "hacker terminal."


Want me to adjust the accent color, add more glow effects, or tweak anything else?

now, how to upload it on github and paste link in my resume.

Reasoning

Here's the quick path:


1. Push to GitHub:

  • Create a new repo on GitHub named eshashahid.github.io (replace with your actual GitHub username)
  • Rename the portfolio.html file to index.html
  • Upload it to that repo (drag and drop works on github.com)


2. Enable GitHub Pages:

  • Go to repo Settings → Pages
  • Under "Source," pick Deploy from a branch → main → / (root)
  • Hit Save. In ~1 min your site will be live at https://yourusername.github.io


3. Add to your resume:

  • Drop the link under your contact details or name, something like: Portfolio: yourusername.github.io


That's it. Free hosting, custom domain support later if you want it. The .github.io URL is clean enough for a resume as-is.
